name: Remove fairings — full sport bike
description: Remove all bodywork on a fully-faired sport bike (CBR, GSX-R, ZX, R1/R6) to access engine, electrical, or cooling components. Includes side fairings, belly pan, upper cowl, and tank shrouds.

name: Remove fairings — half-faired sport touring
description: Remove partial fairing on sport-touring bikes (VFR, FJR, Concours). Less panels than full sport bike but often more complex mounting.

name: Remove tank — standard procedure
description: Disconnect fuel line, vacuum line (carb), electrical connectors, and remove tank. Required for access to airbox, carbs/throttle bodies, wiring harness, and top of engine.

name: Remove seat and side covers
description: Remove seat, side covers, and battery access panels. Required for electrical work, battery, regulator, and fuse access.

name: Remove exhaust system — full
description: Remove complete exhaust system (headers + mid-pipe + muffler). Required for engine removal, cylinder head access, or exhaust gasket replacement.

name: Remove exhaust — slip-on only
description: Remove slip-on muffler section only. Required for rear wheel access on some models or muffler replacement.

name: Drain and refill coolant
description: Drain cooling system, perform work, refill and bleed air from system. Required for any liquid-cooled engine work that opens the cooling system.

name: Drain and refill engine oil
description: Drain engine oil, perform work, refill with fresh oil and new filter. Required when opening crankcases, primary cover, or any oil-wetted component.

name: Remove rear wheel
description: Lift rear, remove axle, remove wheel. Required for tire change, sprocket replacement, brake work, or swingarm bearing access.

name: Remove front wheel
description: Lift front, remove axle, remove wheel. Required for tire change, fork seal replacement, brake work, or steering bearing access.

name: Remove carburetors — inline-4
description: Disconnect throttle cables, choke cable, fuel lines, vacuum lines. Remove 4-carb rack as a unit. Access usually requires tank removal first.

name: Remove carburetors — V-twin or single
description: Disconnect throttle cable, fuel line, remove 1-2 carbs. Simpler than inline-4 but may require tank removal.

name: Carburetor synchronization
description: Connect vacuum gauges, warm engine to operating temp, balance carbs. Required after any carb removal, rebuild, or as standalone tune-up.

name: Reassemble fairings — full sport bike
description: Reinstall all bodywork, align panels, secure all fasteners. Includes test fit and final torque.

name: Final inspection and test ride
description: Post-repair safety inspection: fluid levels, fastener torque, brake test, test ride for verification. Standard completion step for any repair.

name: Initial diagnostic scan and inspection
description: Visual inspection, DTC scan (if equipped), symptom verification, and initial assessment before repair begins.

name: Electrical diagnosis — trace and test
description: Systematic electrical fault tracing: voltage checks, continuity tests, connector inspection. For intermittent or complex electrical issues.

name: Lift/stand setup and cleanup
description: Position bike on lift or stands, secure, and post-job cleanup of workspace.
